items.title: Spirits ferry replacement project cost $717m more than planned

children.content: The Spirit of Tasmania ferry replacement program has cost $717 million more than was forecast in the original business case, a parliamentary inquiry has heard.

gallery.altText: Workers helping to manoeuvre large steel gantry into place at construction site.
A large red and white ferry with SPIRIT OF TASMANIA on it sailing through a bay.
Aerial view of a port facility under construction.

A large red and white ferry with SPIRIT OF TASMANIA on it sailing through a bay.

items.title: Ship owner ordered to pay after 2022 crash sinks tugboats

children.content: A shipping company's attempt to avoid paying millions of dollars to Tasmania's port authority over the sinking of two tugboats in Devonport has been overturned by Australia's High Court.

image.altText: A tug boat sinking in a river, with the cabin still above water

items.title: This duck was 'effectively wiped out' from NZ. There are fears Tasmania's next

children.content: The crossbreeding of Pacific black ducks with introduced species has devastated the native species in New Zealand — and conservationists say there's a risk that's happening in Tasmania too.

image.altText: Ducks

items.title: LISTEN: How will TT-Line manage its growing debt?

children.content: A parliamentary inquiry has heard TT-Line's ferry replacement program has cost $717 million more than was forecast when it began 2018. Tasmania Mornings presenter Leon Compton asks the company's chair how it will weather the storm.

gallery.altText: A large red and white ferry.

thumbnail.altText: A large red and white ferry.

items.title: Starmer faces more calls to quit as ministers resign

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer is striking a defiant tone despite a growing number of his Labour colleagues urging him to step down.

image.altText: Keir Starmer striking a neutral expression on a grey background.

items.title: Australian-born MP at centre of 'palace coup' plot to knife British PM

children.content: Up until the past few days, Catherine West kept a low profile, as far as politicians go. Most Brits would never have come across the Australian-born, London-based MP before, but that all changed at the weekend.

image.altText: A woman smiles at the camera.

images.altText: Trump waves to reporters while he boards Air Force One on May 12.

articles.title: Australia insists support for UAE does not involve action against Iran

children.content: The Albanese government insists Australia is not involved in any "offensive action against Iran" amid reports the UAE, which had been supplied a Wedgetail aircraft, carried out military strikes against the country.

image.altText: A large, grey RAAF aircraft with a huge dorsal fin, in a blue sky.

children.content: US President Donald Trump says he does not need help from Beijing to end the war with Iran but says he will talk at length with Chinese President Xi Jinping when they hold a summit this week. Follow live.

items.title: Trump labels critical news reports on Iran 'virtual treason'

updates.title: Australia joins UK, French counterparts in meeting over Strait of Hormuz
Lebanese authorities estimate 380 killed in Israeli attacks since ceasefire began
Kuwait says four people affiliated with Iran's IRGC arrested trying to enter by sea

items.title: NT government announces details of review into Kumanjayi Little Baby’s situation

children.content: The NT government has revealed the scope of a review into the territory's child protection system, and announced that it will be led by retired NSW police commissioner Karen Webb and long-standing territory public servant Greg Shanahan.

image.altText: Flowers on a fence with a note reading fly high baby girl

items.title: British paratroopers drop onto remote island after suspected hantavirus case

children.content: The UK has ordered 10 people from remote island territories go into isolation, while parachuting in medical supplies, clinicians over suspected hantavirus case.

image.altText: A paratrooper with equipment strapped to his front jumps out of an airplane.

items.title: 'Toxic': Plan to build 91-storey Trump Tower on Gold Coast scrapped

children.content: The branded tower proposed for Surfers Paradise would have been Australia's tallest building.

gallery.altText: A skyline with a very tall Trump Tower in the middle.

thumbnail.altText: A skyline with a very tall Trump Tower in the middle.

items.title: Emergency department nurse faces deportation due to son's condition

children.content: A Victorian emergency department nurse and her family face deportation before the end of the month due to her six-year-old son's developmental delay.

image.altText: A woman and man hold two young children.

items.title: How fuel prices have impacted car sales

children.content: Electric vehicles now represent 16 per cent of new car sales in Australia, in a growing trend coinciding with record fuel prices.

image.altText: A line of vehicles in a car park

items.title: Voss says Carlton in a better place than when he took charge

children.content: Michael Voss has struck a defiant tone after leaving Carlton, insistent the AFL club is in better shape than when he started as coach.

image.altText: AFL coach walking off the field, looking down at his watch

items.title: Three-storey seniors unit block a threat to 'peaceful' street, locals say

children.content: New public housing units built specifically for seniors are under construction in Sydney's south-west, despite opposition from some residents.

image.altText: A graphic showing half: a render of an apartment, and the other half: a mostly renovated apartment in real life.

items.title: Wil Anderson hates advertising. So why does he host a show about it?

children.content: It's been 18 years of hosting Gruen for Wil Anderson, but the self-confessed advertising cynic says the topic is more relevant than ever.

gallery.altText: Wil Anderson in a suit smiling on a television set with a white mug in front of him.

thumbnail.altText: Wil Anderson in a suit smiling on a television set with a white mug in front of him.

items.title: The pianist fighting the MSO over right to bring whole self to stage

children.content: In 2024, Jayson Gillham's show was cancelled for his on-stage comments about Palestine. Now he brings a legal suit and a new recital tour with a Palestinian Jordanian artist.

image.altText: Jayson Gillham, Australian man, 39, in a blazer and white shirt, concentrates as he plays piano in a concert hall.

items.title: Quiz: Dig out your passport, it's time for a whirlwind world trip

children.content: Where was the world's first permanent commercial bungee jumping site set up? And which country is home to the historic city of Timbuktu?

image.altText: A man in mid-air during a bungee jump, with bright blue sky and a fluffy white cloud in the background.

items.title: The name of this health condition was 'very inaccurate'. Now it's changed

children.content: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) has been renamed polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS), a move health experts say will lead to faster diagnoses and better treatment for women of all ages.

gallery.altText: Girl lays on a bed looking at diagram.

thumbnail.altText: Girl lays on a bed looking at diagram.

items.title: Police attend 160 family violence call-outs in Melbourne's south in one week

children.content: Police say a man who threatened to kill his wife with a hammer and tried to set fire to a property was just one of dozens of arrests for family violence in a single week in the Dandenong area.

image.altText: Unidentifiable man and woman arguing

items.title: High Court hears bid to overturn block on 22-year Mount Pleasant mine extension

children.content: The High Court will hear a major climate change case that could affect mining approvals, as the operator of the Mount Pleasant coal mine seeks to overturn a ruling blocking the project's extension.

image.altText: A crowd of women and a few men hold signs protesting coal mines in the Hunter Valley

items.title: Record payout after billionaire built mine without traditional owners' permission

children.content: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to pay $150 million to WA traditional owners after mining their land without permission.

image.altText: A composite image of Andrew Forrest (left) and Michael Woodley situated at a Fortescue mine in WA.

items.title: 'We are heartbroken': NBA in shock after death of Grizzlies forward

children.content: Memphis Grizzlies forward Clarke has died, the team, his agency and the NBA have confirmed.

image.altText: NBA player in street clothes walking through the corridors of an arena before a match

items.title: 'Something catastrophic is going to happen': NT mayor on car thefts spike

children.content: Katherine's mayor says residents feel less safe than they did a year ago and is calling on Northern Territory Chief Minister Lia Finocchiaro to hold an "urgent" roundtable on crime in the Top End town.

items.title: Aussie EPL dream ends and tempers flare over Southampton spying allegation

children.content: Southampton has defeated Middlesbrough to advance to the EFL Championship play-off final after a tense and heated two-leg semifinal.

items.title: Another Aussie out of Giro as UAE Team Emirates hits back

children.content: Jhonatan Narváez dedicated his Giro d'Italia stage four victory to his fallen UAE Team Emirates colleagues as another Aussie fell by the wayside as the race hit Italian soil.

items.title: Government accused of 'burying' response to landmark gambling report

children.content: A long-awaited response to an inquiry that called for a gambling advertising ban has been tabled on budget day, prompting accusations the government is trying to bury the document.

items.title: Jacinta Nampijinpa Price leads emotional tributes for Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Senator Jacinta Nampijinpa Price weeps through an impassioned tribute to her niece, Kumanjayi Little Baby, in federal parliament, amid a push to broaden the focus of an inquiry announced in the wake of her alleged murder.

image.altText: An Indigenous politician giving a speech inside red Senate chambers, she is crying as she holds a tissue to her face.

items.title: Cummins top scores as Sunrisers bowled out for 86 in IPL

items.title: Man injured while allegedly blowing up ATM at Queen Victoria Market

children.content: Police say a man is under police guard in a Melbourne hospital after he allegedly blew up an ATM on Wednesday morning.

image.altText: An ATM in pieces.

items.title: LIV Golf has triggered a turf war in Adelaide's park lands — here are the battlelines
